original_thesis: The 'say exactly what you mean' stack. Curse-of-knowledge is the core problem (experts over-assume shared context); Feynman technique forces genuine understanding; simplification removes noise; false-precision-avoidance prevents spurious accuracy; cognitive-load theory explains why clarity matters (limited processing bandwidth).
corrected_thesis: The 'maximum signal, minimum noise' stack. Cognitive-load theory explains why receivers have limited bandwidth; simplification reduces noise; Occam's razor cuts unnecessary complexity; false-precision-avoidance prevents spurious accuracy that creates false confidence; Pareto principle focuses on the 20% of content that carries 80% of the meaning; prioritization orders what to say first. This stack produces communication that transfers the essential insight without overwhelming or misleading.
what_this_stack_defeats: Expert jargon, false precision, information overload, burying the lead
notes: Original thesis mentioned curse-of-knowledge and Feynman-technique — not members. Actual members form a purer 'editorial compression' stack. The Occam's + Pareto + simplification trio is particularly coherent.
